sn12.1:1.2: at one time, the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta's Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika's park.
sn12.1:1.8: Listen to that, attend carefully, and I will speak.”
sn12.1:2.12: with birth as condition, old age and death, sorrow, lamentation, *dukkha*, distress, and anguish come to be.
sn12.1:2.13: Such is the arising of this whole mass of *dukkha*.
sn12.1:2.2: With blindness [*avijjā*] as condition, *bhikkhus*, there are *saṅkhāras*;
sn12.1:2.5: with *nāmarūpa* as condition, the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*];
sn12.1:2.6: with the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*] as condition, contact;
sn12.1:3.1: But with the remainderless fading away and cessation of blindness [*avijjā*], there is the cessation of *saṅkhāras*;
sn12.1:3.10: with the cessation of *bhava*, the cessation of birth;
sn12.1:3.11: with the cessation of birth, old age and death, sorrow, lamentation, *dukkha*, distress, and anguish cease.
sn12.1:3.12: Such is the cessation of this whole mass of *dukkha*.”
sn12.1:3.14: Satisfied, those *bhikkhus* delighted in the *Bhagavā*'s statement.
sn12.1:3.2: with the cessation of *saṅkhāras*, the cessation of *viññāṇa*;
sn12.1:3.3: with the cessation of *viññāṇa*, the cessation of *nāmarūpa*;
sn12.1:3.4: with the cessation of *nāmarūpa*, the cessation of the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*];
sn12.1:3.5: with the cessation of the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*], the cessation of contact;
sn12.1:3.6: with the cessation of contact, the cessation of *vedanā*;
sn12.1:3.7: with the cessation of *vedanā*, the cessation of *taṇhā*;
sn12.1:3.8: with the cessation of *taṇhā*, the cessation of *upādāna*;
sn12.1:3.9: with the cessation of *upādāna*, the cessation of *bhava*;
